message:
fix(robot,worktrees): correct unread mail accounting and isolate worktree directories by session

Two independent bugs found via exploratory flow tracing and fresh-eyes
codebase archaeology:

1. Agent Mail unread counts included read messages (bd-05bxf)

   The robot's mail summary pipeline had a systematic bug where every
   inbox message was counted as "unread" regardless of its ReadAt field:

   - internal/robot/adapters/work_coordination.go: summarizeMail()
     unconditionally incremented Unread and UrgentUnread for every message
     in the inbox. Now the counters only increment when msg.ReadAt == nil,
     while PendingAck (which is independent of read status) still counts
     all messages that require acknowledgement.

   - internal/robot/robot.go: Four separate call sites in GetMail() used
     tally.Total where they meant tally.Unread when populating the
     UnreadCount field for agent entries. The root cause was that
     inboxTally had no Unread field at all — it only tracked Total,
     Urgent, and PendingAck. Added the Unread field and rewired
     getInboxTally() to skip read messages when counting unread/urgent.

   - internal/robot/robot.go: countInbox() returned len(msgs) without
     filtering, so the sidebar unread badge counted all messages. Now
     iterates and counts only messages with ReadAt == nil.

   Added regression tests in both packages that construct inboxes with a
   mix of read and unread messages and verify the counters match
   expectations.

2. Worktree session isolation collision (bd-jtccc)

   The worktree manager used session-scoped branch names
   (ntm/<session>/<agent>) but stored worktrees at a flat path
   (.ntm/worktrees/<agent>). When two sessions used the same agent name,
   they would collide on disk: CreateForAgent would find the other
   session's directory and skip git worktree creation, ListWorktrees
   would return the wrong session's worktrees, and Cleanup could remove
   another session's worktrees entirely.

   - Introduced three path helper methods (worktreesRoot, sessionRoot,
     worktreePath) that namespace the on-disk layout as
     .ntm/worktrees/<session>/<agent>, matching the branch naming scheme.

   - Updated CreateForAgent, ListWorktrees, RemoveWorktree, Cleanup,
     GetWorktreeForAgent, and isValidWorktree to use the helpers instead
     of hardcoded flat paths.

   - Cleanup now removes the session directory when empty, then
     removes the shared worktrees root only if it is also empty,
     preventing accidental removal of sibling sessions' data.

   - Fixed minor whitespace issues in gitCmd() calls (extra space before
     first argument).

   Added two new regression tests:
   - TestListWorktrees_IsolatedBySession verifies that two managers with
     different sessions each see only their own worktrees.
   - TestCleanup_DoesNotRemoveOtherSessionWorktrees verifies that
     cleaning up one session leaves the other intact.

   Updated existing tests to use the new helper methods instead of
   constructing paths manually.

Also closes bd-1dhfh (Ollama refresh preservation) which was verified
as already fixed in the current tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Snapshot mail totals, per-agent unread counts, urgent counts, and pending-ack counts currently include read messages.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:49:22.392230459Z","created_by":"BlackMaple","updated_at":"2026-03-31T17:49:22.392230459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-08zhh","title":"Scope idempotent replay to request shape and preserve replay metadata","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found that internal/serve idempotency middleware caches responses only by raw Idempotency-Key. Reusing the same key across different methods or routes can replay the wrong response body, and replayed bodies can carry stale request_id metadata that no longer matches headers. Scope cache entries to the request shape, preserve cached replay headers/metadata, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonAspen","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:43:43.511185181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:48:52.100269527Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:48:52.100030339Z","close_reason":"Scoped idempotent replay by request shape, preserved replay request metadata, added regressions, and verified with rch build + idempotency serve tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-103a","title":"Fix agent status showing 'unknown' instead of actual state","description":"## Problem
Agent status in the detail panel shows "unknown" instead of the actual agent state (working, idle, waiting_input, etc.).

## Screenshot Evidence
Detail panel shows:
```
Status
  • unknown
```

## Code Locations
- internal/status/detector.go - UnifiedDetector implementation
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - StatusUpdateMsg handling (line 1192+)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/detail.go - Detail panel rendering

## Possible Causes
1. **Status detection timeout** - Detector may be timing out before getting status
2. **Pane parsing failure** - Unable to parse pane content for status indicators
3. **Race condition** - Status fetch completing after pane data is stale
4. **Missing status patterns** - New agent output patterns not recognized
5. **Async timing** - Status update arriving before pane data is set

## Investigation Steps
1. Enable debug logging in status detector
2. Check if pane content is being captured correctly
3. Verify status patterns match current agent output
4. Check timing of StatusUpdateMsg vs pane data updates

## Diagnostic Code
\`\`\`go
func (d *UnifiedDetector) DetectStatus(pane tmux.Pane) status.AgentStatus {
    log.Debug("detecting status", 
        "pane_id", pane.ID,
        "content_len", len(pane.Content),
        "content_preview", truncate(pane.Content, 100))
    
    status := d.detect(pane)
    log.Debug("status detected",
        "pane_id", pane.ID,
        "status", status.State,
        "confidence", status.Confidence)
    
    return status
}
\`\`\`

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// Ensure status is never "unknown" if we have pane content
func (d *UnifiedDetector) detect(pane tmux.Pane) AgentStatus {
    if len(pane.Content) == 0 {
        return AgentStatus{State: "no_content", Confidence: 0}
    }
    
    // Try all detection methods
    if s := d.detectByPrompt(pane); s.Confidence > 0.5 {
        return s
    }
    if s := d.detectByActivity(pane); s.Confidence > 0.5 {
        return s
    }
    if s := d.detectByOutput(pane); s.Confidence > 0.5 {
        return s
    }
    
    // Fallback with reason
    return AgentStatus{
        State: "unrecognized",
        Confidence: 0.1,
        Reason: "no patterns matched",
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status shows actual agent state (working/idle/waiting/etc.)
- [ ] "unknown" only shown when genuinely unable to determine
- [ ] Status updates within 2 seconds of pane change
- [ ] Status confidence indicator shown when low confidence

## Unit Tests
1. TestStatusDetector_WorkingPattern - detects "working" from tool output
2. TestStatusDetector_IdlePattern - detects "idle" from prompt
3. TestStatusDetector_WaitingPattern - detects "waiting_input" from input prompt
4. TestStatusDetector_EmptyPane - returns "no_content" not "unknown"
5. TestStatusDetector_UnrecognizedReturnsReason - fallback includes reason

## E2E Tests
1. Spawn Claude agent - verify status shows "working" during task
2. Complete task - verify status shows "idle" at prompt
3. Ask question - verify status shows "waiting_input"
4. Rapid status changes - verify UI updates correctly

## Logging
- INFO: Log status changes with before/after
- DEBUG: Log detection method used and confidence
- WARN: Log when falling back to unknown/unrecognized
- ERROR: Log detection failures with pane content sample","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:53.261933227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:02:44.090344240Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:02:44.090224424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-103a","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.034992183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1da61","title":"Feature: UBS robot wrapper and assignment-aware routing","description":"## Overview
Expose UBS scans in robot mode and (optionally) route findings to the agent currently assigned to the file.

## Scope
- Robot wrapper that runs `ubs` with JSON output
- Optional routing to assigned agents (if assignment tracking enabled)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stable JSON output from NTM
- Findings can be mapped to assigned agent panes when enabled
- Graceful when UBS missing","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1dfip","title":"Implement verification logic for restart phases","description":"Implement verification logic for each restart phase.

## Verification Points

### 1. Exit Verification
Check pane output for shell prompt return.
Look for: bash/zsh prompt, $ or >, username@hostname pattern.

### 2. Launch Verification  
Check for claude code ready indicators.
Look for: banner text, prompt character, ready state.

### 3. Prompt Delivery Verification
Verify prompt was received.
Look for: echo of sent text in pane output.

## Implementation
Each verification should:
- Poll with reasonable timeout
- Return detailed status (not just bool)
- Capture relevant output for debugging

## --no-verify Flag
Skip verification for faster operation (less safe).
Use when operator knows environment is stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:37.143997287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:53.699602668Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:53.699451924Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dfip","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:37.172679661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1dmet","title":"Task: Implement output capture for ensemble agents in internal/ensemble/capture.go","description":"# Task: Implement Output Capture for Ensemble Agents

## Location
internal/ensemble/capture.go

## Purpose
Capture pane output from each agent in the ensemble and extract the **YAML output schema** for synthesis. Must handle large outputs and malformed YAML gracefully.

## Implementation
```go
package ensemble

type CapturedOutput struct {
    ModeID        string
    PaneName      string
    RawOutput     string
    Parsed        *ModeOutput
    ParseErrors   []error
    CapturedAt    time.Time
    LineCount     int
    TokenEstimate int
}

type OutputCapture struct {
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    maxLines   int  // Default 1000
    validator  *SchemaValidator
}

func (c *OutputCapture) CaptureAll(session *EnsembleSession) ([]CapturedOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) capturePane(pane string) (string, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) extractYAML(raw string) (string, bool)
```

## YAML Extraction Strategy
- Prefer first fenced ```yaml block
- Fallback: detect `thesis:` root line and capture until EOF
- If multiple YAML blocks, choose the largest valid parse

## Integration with Synthesis
- Parsed ModeOutput is passed to OutputCollector
- RawOutput retained for debugging
- Token estimates via internal/tokens package
- **Do not fail if mode_id missing**; OutputCollector will inject from assignment metadata via Normalize

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Captures output from all ensemble panes
- [ ] Extracts YAML schema blocks reliably
- [ ] Handles missing/malformed output gracefully
- [ ] Token estimation for synthesis planning
- [ ] Configurable max lines to capture
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + parse outcomes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:44.994035314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170149202Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170130136Z","close_reason":"Implemented output capture and extraction logic","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dmet","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:58:17.063829813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:09:24.008880566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.268189513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Unit tests for the Fleet Management commands: ntm scale, ntm rebalance, ntm review-queue, and ntm send --batch.

## Test Categories

### Scale Command Tests
```go
func TestScaleDeltaCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    // Current: cc=3, cod=2, gmi=0
    // Target: cc=5, cod=1, gmi=2
    // Expected: cc=+2, cod=-1, gmi=+2
}

func TestScaleAgentSelection(t *testing.T) {
    // Scale down should select:
    // 1. Idle agents first (longest idle)
    // 2. Agents without assignments
    // 3. Agents with completed work
    // 4. NEVER user pane
}

func TestScaleValidation(t *testing.T) {
    // Reject negative target counts
    // Reject invalid agent types
    // Accept zero (scale to none)
}
```

### Rebalance Command Tests
```go
func TestImbalanceScoreCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    // stddev(workloads) / mean(workloads)
    // Equal distribution -> score ~0
    // Highly skewed -> score >0.5
}

func TestRebalanceTransferSuggestions(t *testing.T) {
    // Sources: above-average workload
    // Targets: below-average or idle
    // Respect constraints: no in-progress moves
}

func TestRebalanceDeadAgentHandling(t *testing.T) {
    // Detect dead agent
    // Suggest reassignment for orphaned tasks
}
```

### Review Queue Tests
```go
func TestIdleDetection(t *testing.T) {
    // Not working && not waiting_for_input
    // Last activity > idle_threshold
    // No pending prompt
}

func TestReviewPromptGeneration(t *testing.T) {
    // From completed agent work
    // From git commits since session start
    // From custom templates
}

func TestPromptTemplateExpansion(t *testing.T) {
    // {diff}, {file}, {agent} variables
    // YAML template parsing
}
```

### Batch Send Tests
```go
func TestBatchFileParsing(t *testing.T) {
    // Comment lines (#)
    // Separator (---)
    // Multi-line prompts
    // Empty lines ignored
}

func TestBatchTargeting(t *testing.T) {
    // Round-robin default
    // --filter agent type
    // --agent specific
    // --broadcast all
}

func TestBatchProgress(t *testing.T) {
    // Correct total count
    // Progress tracking
    // Graceful Ctrl+C handling
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All delta calculations tested with edge cases
- [ ] Agent selection algorithm verified
- [ ] Imbalance score formula validated
- [ ] Template parsing handles malformed input
- [ ] Batch file parsing handles edge cases
- [ ] Progress tracking accurate
- [ ] Error cases have specific assertions

## Logging Format
```go
log.Printf("[TEST-UNIT] test=%s status=%s duration_ms=%d", name, status, ms)
